sections.paragraph:
παρενθυμέομαι 1 aor. pass. παρενεθυμήθην (M. Ant. 5, 5, 5; 6, 20, 2; Iambl., In Nicom. p. 83, 15 Pistelli; TAM II/1, 245, 13; POxf 3, 12 [142 a.d.]; Sb 7404, 39 [II a.d.]; Philo) to view some matter as unworthy of serious consideration, disregard, neglect, forget τὶ someth. (Philo, Spec. Leg. 4, 53) of commands Hm 5, 2, 8 (τὴν ἐντολὴν ταύτην); 12, 3, 6 (ταύτας, i.e. ἐντολάς). Referring to a good thought (τὸ καλόν) and w. αὐτό to be supplied make light of Hs 5, 2, 7. W. ref. to the sins of the members of Hermas’ family, w. αὐτάς to be supplied v 2, 3, 1.—DELG s.v. θυμός.

aor. aor. = aorist

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

  1. Ant. M. Ant. = Marcus Aurelius Antoninus, II a.d.—List 5

Iambl Iambl , phil., III–IV a.d.—List 5

TAM TAM = Tituli Asiae Minoris—List 3

POxf POxf = Some Oxford Papyri—List 4

Sb Sb = Sammelbuch griechischer Urkunden aus Aegypten—Lists 3, 4

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

someth. someth. = something

Hm Hm = Mandates

i.e.

i.e. = id est (that is)

I.-E. Indo-European

Hs Hs = Similitudes

ref. ref. = reference(s)

Hermas Hermas , s. H—List 1

v v = Visions

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 775
